### Post by gejr on 2006-11-30

I have an Intel GMA900 integrated graphics card (laptop).

### Post by groggyboy on 2006-11-30
i'm curious too.  i have an intel 915gma card - i get the error when I run beryl (which runs fine) or when i run glxgears, etc.  i also got the error whenever i tried to run WoW.

anyone?

p.s. - a google search of the error turned up all kinds of results, from forums all over.  some suggested that it means certain openGL functions are not supported by the intel graphics card.  one poster at fedoraforums said he switched to the vesa drivers and got rid of the problem.  several posters said it could be safely ignored - everything else works with the intel driver.  but if gejr is having a problem with slowdowns...  yeah, i don't know.  btw, gejr, what are you trying to run from wine?

### Post by dirgeprat on 2006-12-06
I just installed Ubuntu Edgy for the first time and got this error too -- my graphics card is Radeon 9600 and had worked fine under FedoraCore3.

I was able to get rid of these error messages by using Synaptic to update the drivers for my graphics card -- if you search for 'radeon' there is one called xorg-driver-fglrx (and a devel version that gets downloaded at the same time).

Restarted X-server and it worked for me.

Hope this helps,
CC


**UPDATE:**  I've found this morning that this solution is not ideal.  It disables your direct rendering and means things like glxgears won't work.  But it seems to get past the libGL errors, so could be a decent fix if you don't need fancy graphics...

---

### Post by i_dan on 2007-02-16
I've got a GMA950 and I get this error as well.
As regards glxgears and so on have you tried
 
"glxinfo | grep direct"

It should say;
"direct rendering: Yes"

if it says 'no' try adding 

Load    "i2c"

to

Section "Module"
    
with the rest.  This worked for me.
Ian

---

hey,  got the same problem with Intel GMA 950. no problems with beryl or quake 3. just annoying this error message. maybe it will be gone with feisty or someone figures this out?

### Post by Doovoo on 2007-02-28
I'm also experiencing this problem, It's because I'm using the ATI driver instead of fglrx. This is because I'm running AIGLX which isn't supported by fglrx.

Most people in this thread seem to have the same or a similar setup, so, just to let you know, that's what's wrong. Running XGL + fglrx is one of your options, other than that, I don't know.

See [this thread]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=333682").

### Post by sylecn on 2007-04-01
Users with ati radeon should not have this problem. It can be fixed by installing the correct driver. Users with intel GMA and using the default xorg i810 driver have this problem.

To check whether you installed your card properly, run glxgears, the FPS should be over 2000 after a while and glxgears -fullscreen should have FPS over 150. If you get low FPS while running glxgears you should consider the following DRI driver.

The i810 driver included in xorg seemly do NOT include DRI by default. In order to support hardware accelerated 3D, you must have the Direct Rendering Infrastructure (DRI) installed properly.
The homepage of DRI project is at: [http://dri.freedesktop.org/wiki/FrontPage](http://dri.freedesktop.org/wiki/FrontPage)
Go and fetch the lastest drivers.

if you are using i915 or earlier cards you can also go to
DRI Snapshot: [http://dri.freedesktop.org/snapshots/](http://dri.freedesktop.org/snapshots/)
Though these snapshot has stopped updating. 
I don't know whether i945/i965 cards are supported by the snapshots.

Hopefully it is helpful if you want to install the DRI driver.

Even after installed DRI driver, the libGL warning still shows, it is harmless though.

### Post by binary2k2 on 2007-07-03
The errors like: > 3D driver claims to not support visual 0x24 aren't anything "bad" exactly, it's just your graphics card/chip saying that it doesn't support that particular visual effect. It's a hardware limitation, there's nothing you can really do about it.
